stories.title: Documentation accuracy
stories.description: CLAUDE.md, DESIGN.md, PLAN.md, and AGENT.md must match the actual codebase. File paths, CLI commands, architecture descriptions, type definitions, and feature descriptions must be accurate. Recently removed: group parallelism, claims system, file-lock, pid-utils. These must not appear in docs.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md source tree matches actual files on disk
All CLI commands in docs exist in src/cli/index.ts with matching flags
Architecture descriptions match code — no references to removed features (groups, claims, file-lock, pid-utils, parallel story execution)
DESIGN.md describes current convergent model accurately
No stale references to claims.json, ClaimsManager, groupStories, maxConcurrency, or claimTtlMinutes

stories.title: Dead code and obsolete references
stories.description: Find unused imports, unexported functions that are never called, config fields that are defined but never read, type definitions that nothing uses, and any leftover references to recently removed features (claims, file-lock, pid-utils, group parallelism).
acceptanceCriteria: No unused imports in src/
Every exported function in src/ is imported somewhere (src/ or tests/)
Every field in RalphConfig is actually read by application code
No references to removed modules: claims, file-lock, pid-utils, groupStories, getNextStory
No orphaned test helpers or fixtures for removed features

stories.title: Test coverage and quality
stories.description: Every exported function needs at least one test. Critical paths (convergence, round execution, budget limits, circuit breaker, validation) need edge case coverage. Tests should test behavior, not implementation details.
acceptanceCriteria: Every exported function in src/ has at least one test
Convergent loop: re-evaluation, regression detection, convergence exit, no_progress exit all tested
Edge cases tested: empty PRD, corrupt JSON, missing files, zero stories
No tests that only verify internal state without observable behavior

stories.title: Robustness to multiple runs
stories.description: Ralph must handle being run multiple times on the same repo without corruption or confusion. State files from previous runs must not interfere. Leftover .ralph/runs/ directories, stale progress.md, partial state files — all must be handled gracefully.
acceptanceCriteria: Running ralph twice produces independent run state files
Stale progress.md from a previous run does not confuse the current run
Corrupt or partial run-state.json is handled without crashing
The resume command correctly identifies resumable vs non-resumable runs

stories.title: Overengineering and unnecessary complexity
stories.description: Find abstractions that serve only one caller, config options that nobody uses, modules that could be inlined, and layers of indirection that don't earn their keep. The codebase should be as simple as possible for what it does.
acceptanceCriteria: No module exists solely to re-export or wrap a single function
No config option exists that has no practical effect on behavior
ExitDetector, if it exists, is justified — otherwise inline or remove
CircuitBreaker complexity is proportional to its value
No unnecessary abstractions between the loop and its dependencies

stories.title: CLI output and user experience
stories.description: CLI commands should produce clear, helpful output. Error messages should tell the user what went wrong and what to do. Help text should be accurate. The dry-run command should give a useful preview.
acceptanceCriteria: Every CLI command has a --help that accurately describes its options
Error messages include actionable guidance (not just 'failed')
ralph dry-run shows all resolved config and PRD summary
ralph status/cost/logs degrade gracefully when data is missing or partial

stories.title: Design and methodology soundness
stories.description: The convergent loop model must actually converge. Quality gates must be enforced by the loop, not trusted from agent output. The circuit breaker must prevent infinite loops. Budget guardrails must actually stop the loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Convergent mode exits when a round produces zero state changes
Backlog mode exits when all stories pass or zero fixes in a round
Circuit breaker trips on repeated failures and actually stops the loop
Budget limits (maxCostUsd, maxIterations, maxRounds) are checked before each story, not just at round boundaries
Quality gates cannot be bypassed by agent output claiming success
